stops.extraction_target: Reason: the sum of its distinct prime factors

riddle:
1. Seek the film whose title pairs a woman's given name with a nationality; open its page and tell me the film's running time, expressed in whole minutes.

2. How many 1-bits appear if you write the runtime you just found (from clue 1) in binary?

3. There exists a page for the filmmaker who directed that picture; follow the director's article and record the age at death shown there.

4. If you follow the link on the director's page to the cinematic movement he helped lead, look at the movement's article near the top and count how many language editions of the article are listed.

5. The article that chronicles the country's mid‑century political period will help with a unit change; on that page, convert 110 metres into feet and report the integer part (truncate any decimals).

6. In narrative: an actress who appeared in the seed film later merited her own biography; consult her entry and note the age at which she died.

7. The silver‑screen retelling of the Orpheus myth set in Rio will whisper its duration on its film page—what running time in whole minutes does that article's infobox show?

8. (As a brief detour,) convert 15 metres into yards and take the integer portion of that result.

9. Consider this: take the integer you obtained in clue 8 and compute the sum of its distinct prime factors — what number do you get?

10. At last, perform the final arithmetic using only the numeric results you've gathered: compute the absolute difference between clue 2 and clue 6, then take that result modulo 10 — in formula form, evaluate abs(clue 2 - clue 6) % 10. The answer will be a single digit from 0 to 9.

metadata.theme: Tracing Joanna Francesa through its creator and the Cinema Novo movement — linking film, director, performers and Brazil's political and geographic context, with three data-tool stops to quantify runtime, population and terrain.
